content.content:
1	# Navigation Pattern
2	
3	Use this for detail pages, editors, settings, onboarding steps, drill-down flows, and any app with more than one page.
4	
5	## Core Pattern
6	
7	Use `Navigation` plus `NavPathStack`, with destination rendering centralized in `navDestination`.
8	
9	```ts
10	export class RouteNames {
11	  static readonly Detail: string = 'detail'
12	  static readonly Edit: string = 'edit'
13	}
14	
15	export class DetailRouteParams {
16	  itemId: string = ''
17	}
18	
19	@Builder
20	routeBuilder(name: string, param: object) {
21	  if (name === RouteNames.Detail) {
22	    NavDestination() {
23	      DetailPage({ itemId: (param as DetailRouteParams).itemId })
24	    }
25	    .title('Detail')
26	  }
27	}
28	```
29	
30	Push from user actions or explicit methods:
31	
32	```ts
33	private openDetail(item: ItemModel): void {
34	  let params: DetailRouteParams = new DetailRouteParams()
35	  params.itemId = item.id
36	  this.navStack.pushPath({ name: RouteNames.Detail, param: params })
37	}
38	```
39	
40	## Lightweight Page Context
41	
42	When several components or a view model need navigation, wrap `NavPathStack` instead of scattering stack calls. Keep the wrapper local to the app shell unless the existing project already uses `AppStorage` for navigation contexts.
43	
44	For a copyable starting point, read `../snippets/PageContext.ets`.
45	
46	Use it from the shell or a feature VM:
47	
48	```ts
49	private pageContext: PageContext = new PageContext()
50	
51	private openDetail(id: string): void {
52	  let param: DetailRouteParams = new DetailRouteParams()
53	  param.itemId = id
54	  this.pageContext.open({ name: RouteNames.Detail, param })
55	}
56	```
57	
58	## Shared Stack vs Per-Tab Stack
59	
60	Choose the navigation owner before writing pages:
61	
62	- Single primary flow or detail pages that should cover the whole app: use one root `Navigation` and one root `NavPathStack`.
63	- Bottom tabs where each tab has its own detail/editor flow and should preserve history while switching tabs: use one `Navigation` and one `NavPathStack` inside each `TabContent`.
64	- Rare global flows such as login, onboarding, or full-app settings can still live on a root stack above tabs if the product requires them.
65	
66	Per-tab stacks keep back history independent:
67	
68	```ts
69	@Component
70	struct TabShell {
71	  @State private selectedTab: number = 0
72	  private homeStack: NavPathStack = new NavPathStack()
73	  private tasksStack: NavPathStack = new NavPathStack()
74	  private settingsStack: NavPathStack = new NavPathStack()
75	
76	  @Builder
77	  routeBuilder(name: string, param: object) {
78	    if (name === RouteNames.Detail) {
79	      NavDestination() {
80	        DetailPage({ itemId: (param as DetailRouteParams).itemId })
81	      }
82	    }
83	  }
84	
85	  build() {
86	    Tabs({ index: $$this.selectedTab, barPosition: BarPosition.End }) {
87	      TabContent() {
88	        Navigation(this.homeStack) {
89	          HomePage({ navStack: this.homeStack })
90	        }
91	        .navDestination(this.routeBuilder)
92	      }
93	      .tabBar('Home')
94	
95	      TabContent() {
96	        Navigation(this.tasksStack) {
97	          TasksPage({ navStack: this.tasksStack })
98	        }
99	        .navDestination(this.routeBuilder)
100	      }
101	      .tabBar('Tasks')
102	
103	      TabContent() {
104	        Navigation(this.settingsStack) {
105	          SettingsPage({ navStack: this.settingsStack })
106	        }
107	        .navDestination(this.routeBuilder)
108	      }
109	      .tabBar('Settings')
110	    }
111	  }
112	}
113	```
114	
115	Use stack methods intentionally:
116	
117	```ts
118	private resetCurrentTab(): void {
119	  if (this.selectedTab === 0) {
120	    this.homeStack.clear()
121	  } else if (this.selectedTab === 1) {
122	    this.tasksStack.clear()
123	  }
124	}
125	```
126	
127	## Route Parameters
128	
129	- Define named parameter classes with default values.
130	- Pass ids, mode flags, or small immutable data snapshots.
131	- Resolve large data from a service/store by id.
132	- Add a fallback destination for unknown route names during generation.
133	- Keep route names stable across root and per-tab builders; route to services by id instead of copying large records into params.
134	
135	## Choosing Navigation vs Router
136	
137	Prefer `NavPathStack` and `navDestination` for generated apps. Use `@ohos.router` only when the existing app is already structured around router pages or when a required platform flow explicitly depends on it.
138	
139	## Avoid
140	
141	- `pushPath`, `replacePath`, or `pop` inside `aboutToAppear`.
142	- Route pushes hidden in random row components when a page-level method or `PageContext` would expose intent.
143	- Sharing one `NavPathStack` across all tabs when tab history should be independent.
144	- Creating a new `NavPathStack` during `build()` or from a computed getter.
145	- Route params typed as anonymous object literals.
146	- Route names embedded as string literals in many files.
147	- Detail pages that mutate parent list data directly without a clear state ownership path.
148	
149	## Source Notes
150	
151	HarmonyOS Navigation docs describe `Navigation` as a root navigation container and recommend `NavPathStack` with `navDestination` from API 10. `NavPathStack` supports methods such as `pushPath`, `pop`, `clear`, `getAllPathName`, and `size`. The docs also warn that stack operations in `aboutToAppear` happen before page build completion and can cause white screens or failed jumps.
152	
153	The local sample app uses a `PageContext` wrapper around `NavPathStack` in `$HARMONY_SAMPLE_APP_ROOT/common/src/main/ets/routermanager/PageContext.ets`. Reuse the idea, not its app-wide `AppStorage` context layout, unless the target project already has that architecture.
154

1	# State Ownership Pattern
2	
3	Use this before choosing ArkUI decorators or moving state between pages and components.
4	
5	## Defaults
6	
7	- `@State`: local component-owned reactive state.
8	- `@Prop`: parent-to-child input when the child should not mutate the parent value.
9	- `@Link`: parent-owned value that a child is allowed to mutate.
10	- `@Observed` with `@ObjectLink`: object state whose property changes must refresh child UI.
11	- `@Provide` and `@Consume`: section-wide or app-wide shared dependencies. Use sparingly.
12	- Plain private fields: constants, controllers, services, and derived helpers that do not need to trigger UI refresh.
13	
14	## Generated Model Rules
15	
16	Use named classes with defaults:
17	
18	```ts
19	@Observed
20	export class TodoItem {
21	  id: string = ''
22	  title: string = ''
23	  completed: boolean = false
24	}
25	```
26	
27	Avoid anonymous object types in component state:
28	
29	```ts
30	// Avoid for generated ArkTS UI state.
31	@State item: { id: string, title: string } = { id: '', title: '' }
32	```
33	
34	## Parent To Child
35	
36	Read-only child:
37	
38	```ts
39	@Component
40	struct SummaryCard {
41	  @Prop title: string
42	}
43	```
44	
45	Child edits parent primitive:
46	
47	```ts
48	@Component
49	struct SearchBox {
50	  @Link query: string
51	}
52	```
53	
54	Child edits nested object:
55	
56	```ts
57	@Component
58	struct TodoRow {
59	  @ObjectLink item: TodoItem
60	}
61	```
62	
63	## Ownership Decisions
64	
65	- Page owns selected tab, search query, filters, drafts, loading state, and dialog state.
66	- Row owns only transient row-only UI state unless the data change must affect the list.
67	- Services own persistence, data loading, and generated mock repositories.
68	- Root shell owns navigation and app-wide selection only when multiple pages need it.
69	
70	## ViewModel State Shape
71	
72	For pages with async loading, persistence, filtering, or navigation side effects, use a small state plus view-model boundary. Do not copy a large sample-app inheritance tree unless the project already uses one.
73	
74	For a copyable starting point, read `../snippets/StateViewModelSkeleton.ets`.
75	
76	Use `@State private screenState: ItemListState = this.vm.state` at the page level. Use `@ObjectLink` for row/detail components that must observe nested item changes. If list mutations are simple, reassign arrays (`this.items = next`) rather than relying on `push` or `splice` to refresh child UI.
77	
78	Shared render state rule: if child tabs/pages render fields from a shared object, that object's class must be `@Observed` and children must receive it with `@ObjectLink`; otherwise `object.field = value` may build successfully but fail to re-render other tabs/pages.
79	
80	For tabbed apps where multiple pages read and update the same screen state, read `../snippets/SharedStateTabsShell.ets`. Use this shape when a root shell owns a view model, multiple `TabContent` pages display fields from the same state object, and page button clicks must update UI across tabs. The root keeps the view model as a plain command object, owns `@State private appState = this.vm.state`, and passes the observed state to child pages with `@ObjectLink`. Child pages call callbacks such as `onIncrement` instead of receiving a mutable `vm!`. For small demos, `@Observed ViewModel` is acceptable only when the VM is the rendered shared state object; for larger apps, prefer `@Observed AppState` for render data plus a plain VM/controller for commands, services, and persistence.
81	
82	Do not repair component input initialization errors by changing `vm: SomeViewModel` to `vm!: SomeViewModel` when the UI reads mutable fields from that view model. Pick the state owner first:
83	
84	- If the child only reads immutable input, use `@Prop`.
85	- If the child edits a parent primitive or array reference, use `@Link`.
86	- If the child observes fields on a shared object, make that object `@Observed` and pass it with `@ObjectLink`; do not rely on a parent `@State vm` wrapper to observe `vm.field` mutations.
87	- If the child triggers behavior, pass a defaulted callback or a plain service that does not need to refresh UI.
88	
89	## Form Drafts
90	
91	Keep create/edit drafts local to the page or dialog, then submit to a view model or service. This avoids mutating nested fields on a plain controller object while expecting ArkUI to refresh.
92	
93	```ts
94	@State private draftName: string = ''
95	
96	private saveDraft(): void {
97	  let draft: ItemDraft = new ItemDraft()
98	  draft.name = this.draftName.trim()
99	  this.vm.save(draft)
100	}
101	```
102	
103	## Avoid
104	
105	- Uninitialized decorated state.
106	- Mutating nested properties on a plain object and expecting all child UI to refresh.
107	- Passing a plain mutable ViewModel to tab/page children and expecting derived stats, lists, or badges to refresh after another tab changes it.
108	- Plain `AppState` objects passed through many components without `@Observed`, `@ObjectLink`, `@Link`, or explicit state reassignment.
109	- Plain `vm!` or `viewModel!` fields in child pages when the page renders mutable view-model fields.
110	- Passing mutable arrays into many children without a single update path.
111	- Using `@Provide/@Consume` to avoid one or two explicit parameters.
112	- Global mutable singletons for screen state in generated apps.
113	
114	## Source Notes
115	
116	The local sample app uses `@Observed` state classes and VM objects in files such as `$HARMONY_SAMPLE_APP_ROOT/features/mine/src/main/ets/viewmodel/NewContentPageState.ets` and `NewContentVM.ets`. Reuse the small `State + VM + event/method` idea; avoid importing its large app-specific inheritance, breakpoint, and status-bar logic into generated apps.

file.content:
# State Ownership Pattern

Use this before choosing ArkUI decorators or moving state between pages and components.

## Defaults

- `@State`: local component-owned reactive state.
- `@Prop`: parent-to-child input when the child should not mutate the parent value.
- `@Link`: parent-owned value that a child is allowed to mutate.
- `@Observed` with `@ObjectLink`: object state whose property changes must refresh child UI.
- `@Provide` and `@Consume`: section-wide or app-wide shared dependencies. Use sparingly.
- Plain private fields: constants, controllers, services, and derived helpers that do not need to trigger UI refresh.

## Generated Model Rules

Use named classes with defaults:

```ts
@Observed
export class TodoItem {
  id: string = ''
  title: string = ''
  completed: boolean = false
}
```

Avoid anonymous object types in component state:

```ts
// Avoid for generated ArkTS UI state.
@State item: { id: string, title: string } = { id: '', title: '' }
```

## Parent To Child

Read-only child:

```ts
@Component
struct SummaryCard {
  @Prop title: string
}
```

Child edits parent primitive:

```ts
@Component
struct SearchBox {
  @Link query: string
}
```

Child edits nested object:

```ts
@Component
struct TodoRow {
  @ObjectLink item: TodoItem
}
```

## Ownership Decisions

- Page owns selected tab, search query, filters, drafts, loading state, and dialog state.
- Row owns only transient row-only UI state unless the data change must affect the list.
- Services own persistence, data loading, and generated mock repositories.
- Root shell owns navigation and app-wide selection only when multiple pages need it.

## ViewModel State Shape

For pages with async loading, persistence, filtering, or navigation side effects, use a small state plus view-model boundary. Do not copy a large sample-app inheritance tree unless the project already uses one.

For a copyable starting point, read `../snippets/StateViewModelSkeleton.ets`.

Use `@State private screenState: ItemListState = this.vm.state` at the page level. Use `@ObjectLink` for row/detail components that must observe nested item changes. If list mutations are simple, reassign arrays (`this.items = next`) rather than relying on `push` or `splice` to refresh child UI.

Shared render state rule: if child tabs/pages render fields from a shared object, that object's class must be `@Observed` and children must receive it with `@ObjectLink`; otherwise `object.field = value` may build successfully but fail to re-render other tabs/pages.

For tabbed apps where multiple pages read and update the same screen state, read `../snippets/SharedStateTabsShell.ets`. Use this shape when a root shell owns a view model, multiple `TabContent` pages display fields from the same state object, and page button clicks must update UI across tabs. The root keeps the view model as a plain command object, owns `@State private appState = this.vm.state`, and passes the observed state to child pages with `@ObjectLink`. Child pages call callbacks such as `onIncrement` instead of receiving a mutable `vm!`. For small demos, `@Observed ViewModel` is acceptable only when the VM is the rendered shared state object; for larger apps, prefer `@Observed AppState` for render data plus a plain VM/controller for commands, services, and persistence.

Do not repair component input initialization errors by changing `vm: SomeViewModel` to `vm!: SomeViewModel` when the UI reads mutable fields from that view model. Pick the state owner first:

- If the child only reads immutable input, use `@Prop`.
- If the child edits a parent primitive or array reference, use `@Link`.
- If the child observes fields on a shared object, make that object `@Observed` and pass it with `@ObjectLink`; do not rely on a parent `@State vm` wrapper to observe `vm.field` mutations.
- If the child triggers behavior, pass a defaulted callback or a plain service that does not need to refresh UI.

## Form Drafts

Keep create/edit drafts local to the page or dialog, then submit to a view model or service. This avoids mutating nested fields on a plain controller object while expecting ArkUI to refresh.

```ts
@State private draftName: string = ''

private saveDraft(): void {
  let draft: ItemDraft = new ItemDraft()
  draft.name = this.draftName.trim()
  this.vm.save(draft)
}
```

## Avoid

- Uninitialized decorated state.
- Mutating nested properties on a plain object and expecting all child UI to refresh.
- Passing a plain mutable ViewModel to tab/page children and expecting derived stats, lists, or badges to refresh after another tab changes it.
- Plain `AppState` objects passed through many components without `@Observed`, `@ObjectLink`, `@Link`, or explicit state reassignment.
- Plain `vm!` or `viewModel!` fields in child pages when the page renders mutable view-model fields.
- Passing mutable arrays into many children without a single update path.
- Using `@Provide/@Consume` to avoid one or two explicit parameters.
- Global mutable singletons for screen state in generated apps.

## Source Notes

The local sample app uses `@Observed` state classes and VM objects in files such as `$HARMONY_SAMPLE_APP_ROOT/features/mine/src/main/ets/viewmodel/NewContentPageState.ets` and `NewContentVM.ets`. Reuse the small `State + VM + event/method` idea; avoid importing its large app-specific inheritance, breakpoint, and status-bar logic into generated apps.

1	# Lightweight Clients Pattern
2	
3	Use this for generated apps that need mock data, local repositories, persistence shims, or small API-like services without a heavy architecture.
4	
5	## Intent
6	
7	Keep views declarative and keep data policy outside `build()`. Generated apps should have a small repository/client layer even when the data is mocked.
8	
9	## Minimal Shape
10	
11	```ts
12	export class ItemRepository {
13	  private items: ItemModel[] = []
14	
15	  constructor() {
16	    this.items = this.createInitialItems()
17	  }
18	
19	  listItems(): ItemModel[] {
20	    return this.items.slice()
21	  }
22	
23	  search(query: string, scope: SearchScope): ItemModel[] {
24	    let normalized: string = query.trim().toLowerCase()
25	    return this.items.filter((item: ItemModel) => {
26	      return item.title.toLowerCase().includes(normalized)
27	    })
28	  }
29	
30	  private createInitialItems(): ItemModel[] {
31	    return [
32	      new ItemModel('1', 'First item'),
33	      new ItemModel('2', 'Second item')
34	    ]
35	  }
36	}
37	```
38	
39	## JSON Preferences Store
40	
41	Use this for small local CRUD/settings data. Pass `Context` from a page or app shell; do not require global `AppStorage` for a generated small app.
42	
43	For a copyable starting point, read `../snippets/JsonPreferenceStore.ets`.
44	
45	Map parsed JSON into named model classes instead of exposing dynamic records to UI state:
46	
47	```ts
48	private toItem(raw: Record<string, string | number | boolean>): ItemModel {
49	  let item: ItemModel = new ItemModel()
50	  item.id = String(raw['id'] ?? '')
51	  item.title = String(raw['title'] ?? '')
52	  item.createdAt = Number(raw['createdAt'] ?? 0)
53	  return item
54	}
55	
56	async list(): Promise<ItemModel[]> {
57	  let json: string = this.store.getString('items', '[]')
58	  try {
59	    return (JSON.parse(json) as object[]).map((raw: object) => {
60	      return this.toItem(raw as Record<string, string | number | boolean>)
61	    })
62	  } catch (_) {
63	    return []
64	  }
65	}
66	```
67	
68	## Page Usage
69	
70	```ts
71	private repository: ItemRepository = new ItemRepository()
72	@State private items: ItemModel[] = []
73	
74	aboutToAppear(): void {
75	  if (this.items.length === 0) {
76	    this.items = this.repository.listItems()
77	  }
78	}
79	```
80	
81	## Rules
82	
83	- Keep repositories/services as plain classes unless they need ArkUI observation.
84	- Return copies for arrays when callers should not mutate internal state directly.
85	- Wrap preferences operations in `try/catch`; static checks often flag them as throwing.
86	- Await `flush()` when the surrounding method is async, or intentionally encapsulate it in a fire-and-forget helper with error handling.
87	- Put generated fixtures in repository/model files, not `build()`.
88	- Keep async/network boundaries in services; keep UI phase state in pages or stores.
89	- Use named models and enums for data, not anonymous objects.
90	- Prefer explicit setter methods or full typed model assignment over TypeScript utility types such as `Partial<T>` plus object spread. ArkTS strict checks reject object spread in ordinary object literals.
91	
92	## Avoid
93	
94	- Global mutable arrays as app state.
95	- Fetching, sorting, or generating mock data inside `build()`.
96	- A large view model for a screen with simple local state.
97	- Client classes that know about ArkUI components.
98	- Object spread updates such as `{ ...this.settings, ...patch }` in generated ArkTS services.
99	- `Record<string, unknown>` or `any` in generated ArkTS parsing paths; use explicit DTO/model mapping.
100	
101	## Source Notes
102	
103	This mirrors the SwiftUI lightweight-client role for HarmonyOS: a small swappable data boundary that supports generated apps, tests, and later replacement by real APIs.
104	
105	The local sample app wraps preferences with try/catch in `$HARMONY_SAMPLE_APP_ROOT/common/src/main/ets/storagemanager/PreferenceManager.ets`. Reuse the protection and JSON boundary, not its fixed singleton, logger, or global UIContext.
106

1	# Dialogs And Overlays Pattern
2	
3	Use this for confirmations, custom editors, pickers, action sheets, popups, menus, and transient feedback.
4	
5	## Decision
6	
7	- Simple confirmation: `AlertDialog`.
8	- List of actions: `ActionSheet` or a menu.
9	- Date/time/text picker: platform picker dialog.
10	- Custom modal content: `CustomDialog`.
11	- Lightweight contextual hint: popup or tips control.
12	- Short feedback: toast/prompt action where available in the project.
13	
14	## Custom Dialog Shape
15	
16	`CustomDialogController` should be a member of a `@Component` and should open a `@CustomDialog` component.
17	
18	```ts
19	@CustomDialog
20	struct RenameDialog {
21	  controller: CustomDialogController
22	  @Link name: string
23	
24	  build() {
25	    Column({ space: 12 }) {
26	      Text('Rename')
27	      TextInput({ text: this.name })
28	        .id('rename-input')
29	        .onChange((value: string) => {
30	          this.name = value
31	        })
32	      Button('Done')
33	        .id('rename-done-button')
34	        .onClick(() => this.controller.close())
35	    }
36	    .padding(20)
37	  }
38	}
39	```
40	
41	```ts
42	@State private draftName: string = ''
43	private renameDialog: CustomDialogController = new CustomDialogController({
44	  builder: RenameDialog({
45	    name: $draftName
46	  })
47	})
48	```
49	
50	## State Model
51	
52	For multiple modal choices, prefer one state field:
53	
54	```ts
55	enum ActiveDialog {
56	  None,
57	  DeleteConfirm,
58	  Rename
59	}
60	```
61	
62	Open dialogs through methods such as `openRename(itemId: string)` and `confirmDelete()`, so state setup stays outside `build()`.
63	
64	## Sheet State
65	
66	For bottom sheets or half-modal editors, keep one boolean or enum as the source of truth and reset it in the dismiss callback.
67	
68	For copyable starting points, read `../snippets/BindSheetEditor.ets` or `../snippets/CustomDialogForm.ets`.
69	
70	If `bindSheet` support is uncertain for the target SDK, use a `CustomDialog` form rather than hand-rolling a full-screen overlay unless the visual contract specifically requires it.
71	
72	## Rules
73	
74	- Dialog dynamic content should use linked or consumed state when it must refresh.
75	- Close an existing custom dialog before replacing a global or shared controller.
76	- Keep destructive confirmation copy short and action-specific.
77	- Add ids to dialog inputs and primary/secondary buttons.
78	
79	## Avoid
80	
81	- Many unrelated booleans such as `showDelete`, `showRename`, `showPicker`.
82	- `@Prop` or `@ObjectLink` as the only path for dialog content that must update while open.
83	- Directly closing the parent dialog from a nested dialog launcher.
84	- Recreating controllers inside `build()`.
85	- Keeping sheet state only in a service or plain controller object when the UI must react to open/close changes.
86	
87	## Source Notes
88	
89	HarmonyOS CustomDialog docs require the controller to be used with `@CustomDialog` and `@Component`, note that controller parameters do not dynamically refresh, and recommend `@Link` or `@Consume` for dynamic dialog content.
90	
91	The local sample app uses `.bindSheet(show, builder, { onWillDisappear })` in `$HARMONY_SAMPLE_APP_ROOT/features/devpractices/src/main/ets/component/SampleCard.ets`; reuse the state-reset pattern without copying its web-sheet-specific details.
92

file.content:
# Dialogs And Overlays Pattern

Use this for confirmations, custom editors, pickers, action sheets, popups, menus, and transient feedback.

## Decision

- Simple confirmation: `AlertDialog`.
- List of actions: `ActionSheet` or a menu.
- Date/time/text picker: platform picker dialog.
- Custom modal content: `CustomDialog`.
- Lightweight contextual hint: popup or tips control.
- Short feedback: toast/prompt action where available in the project.

## Custom Dialog Shape

`CustomDialogController` should be a member of a `@Component` and should open a `@CustomDialog` component.

```ts
@CustomDialog
struct RenameDialog {
  controller: CustomDialogController
  @Link name: string

  build() {
    Column({ space: 12 }) {
      Text('Rename')
      TextInput({ text: this.name })
        .id('rename-input')
        .onChange((value: string) => {
          this.name = value
        })
      Button('Done')
        .id('rename-done-button')
        .onClick(() => this.controller.close())
    }
    .padding(20)
  }
}
```

```ts
@State private draftName: string = ''
private renameDialog: CustomDialogController = new CustomDialogController({
  builder: RenameDialog({
    name: $draftName
  })
})
```

## State Model

For multiple modal choices, prefer one state field:

```ts
enum ActiveDialog {
  None,
  DeleteConfirm,
  Rename
}
```

Open dialogs through methods such as `openRename(itemId: string)` and `confirmDelete()`, so state setup stays outside `build()`.

## Sheet State

For bottom sheets or half-modal editors, keep one boolean or enum as the source of truth and reset it in the dismiss callback.

For copyable starting points, read `../snippets/BindSheetEditor.ets` or `../snippets/CustomDialogForm.ets`.

If `bindSheet` support is uncertain for the target SDK, use a `CustomDialog` form rather than hand-rolling a full-screen overlay unless the visual contract specifically requires it.

## Rules

- Dialog dynamic content should use linked or consumed state when it must refresh.
- Close an existing custom dialog before replacing a global or shared controller.
- Keep destructive confirmation copy short and action-specific.
- Add ids to dialog inputs and primary/secondary buttons.

## Avoid

- Many unrelated booleans such as `showDelete`, `showRename`, `showPicker`.
- `@Prop` or `@ObjectLink` as the only path for dialog content that must update while open.
- Directly closing the parent dialog from a nested dialog launcher.
- Recreating controllers inside `build()`.
- Keeping sheet state only in a service or plain controller object when the UI must react to open/close changes.

## Source Notes

HarmonyOS CustomDialog docs require the controller to be used with `@CustomDialog` and `@Component`, note that controller parameters do not dynamically refresh, and recommend `@Link` or `@Consume` for dynamic dialog content.

The local sample app uses `.bindSheet(show, builder, { onWillDisappear })` in `$HARMONY_SAMPLE_APP_ROOT/features/devpractices/src/main/ets/component/SampleCard.ets`; reuse the state-reset pattern without copying its web-sheet-specific details.

1	# Forms And Controls Pattern
2	
3	Use this for create/edit pages, settings, search filters, profile forms, onboarding, and preference screens.
4	
5	## Default Layout
6	
7	Use `Scroll` plus a structured `Column` for input-heavy pages.
8	
9	```ts
10	Scroll() {
11	  Column({ space: 16 }) {
12	    this.textField('Name', this.name, 'profile-name-input')
13	    this.toggleRow('Enable reminders', this.enableReminders, 'reminders-toggle')
14	
15	    Button('Save')
16	      .id('save-button')
17	      .enabled(this.canSave())
18	      .onClick(() => this.save())
19	  }
20	  .width('100%')
21	  .padding(16)
22	}
23	```
24	
25	## Control Choices
26	
27	- Text entry: `TextInput` or `TextArea`.
28	- Binary setting: `Toggle`, `Checkbox`, or `Switch` style `Toggle`.
29	- Multiple exclusive options: `Radio`, `Select`, or segmented control.
30	- Numeric value: `Slider`, `Counter`, `TextInput` with validation, or picker.
31	- Command: real `Button`, not a styled `Text`.
32	- Destructive command: button plus confirmation dialog when user data changes.
33	
34	## State And Validation
35	
36	- Keep editable values in local `@State` on the form page.
37	- Use named validation methods such as `private canSave(): boolean`.
38	- Store field-level error text in state if the UI displays it.
39	- Convert and validate input before persisting or navigating away.
40	- For parent-owned edits, use a draft copy in local state and commit through a callback or service.
41	- Live control values displayed on the same screen must be owned by page `@State` or child `@Link`.
42	- In `onChange`, update that reactive value first, then call controllers, stores, persistence, or async services.
43	- Do not rely on async shared-state refresh to update the label beside the active control.
44	
45	## Builders
46	
47	Small local field builders are acceptable when they reduce repeated label/control layout:
48	
49	```ts
50	@Builder
51	textField(label: string, value: string, componentId: string) {
52	  Column({ space: 6 }) {
53	    Text(label)
54	    TextInput({ text: value })
55	      .id(componentId)
56	      .onChange((next: string) => {
57	        if (componentId === 'profile-name-input') {
58	          this.name = next
59	        }
60	      })
61	  }
62	  .width('100%')
63	}
64	```
65	
66	For larger fields with independent state or multiple callbacks, use a custom component instead of a builder.
67	
68	For sliders with nearby live text, use `live-slider-control.md`; do not hide live value ownership in a builder parameter.
69	
70	## Avoid
71	
72	- Styled `Text` or `Row` acting as controls when a native control exists.
73	- Anonymous object literals for form state.
74	- One large `onClick` that validates, mutates, persists, shows dialogs, and navigates.
75	- Editing parent state directly from many nested controls without `@Link` or an explicit callback.
76	- `Slider({ value })` or `TextInput({ text: value })` in a `@Builder` when `value` is a plain parameter and nearby text is expected to refresh while editing.
77	- Missing empty/error/disabled states for save and submit actions.
78

# Forms And Controls Pattern

Use this for create/edit pages, settings, search filters, profile forms, onboarding, and preference screens.

## Default Layout

Use `Scroll` plus a structured `Column` for input-heavy pages.

```ts
Scroll() {
  Column({ space: 16 }) {
    this.textField('Name', this.name, 'profile-name-input')
    this.toggleRow('Enable reminders', this.enableReminders, 'reminders-toggle')

    Button('Save')
      .id('save-button')
      .enabled(this.canSave())
      .onClick(() => this.save())
  }
  .width('100%')
  .padding(16)
}
```

## Control Choices

- Text entry: `TextInput` or `TextArea`.
- Binary setting: `Toggle`, `Checkbox`, or `Switch` style `Toggle`.
- Multiple exclusive options: `Radio`, `Select`, or segmented control.
- Numeric value: `Slider`, `Counter`, `TextInput` with validation, or picker.
- Command: real `Button`, not a styled `Text`.
- Destructive command: button plus confirmation dialog when user data changes.

## State And Validation

- Keep editable values in local `@State` on the form page.
- Use named validation methods such as `private canSave(): boolean`.
- Store field-level error text in state if the UI displays it.
- Convert and validate input before persisting or navigating away.
- For parent-owned edits, use a draft copy in local state and commit through a callback or service.
- Live control values displayed on the same screen must be owned by page `@State` or child `@Link`.
- In `onChange`, update that reactive value first, then call controllers, stores, persistence, or async services.
- Do not rely on async shared-state refresh to update the label beside the active control.

## Builders

Small local field builders are acceptable when they reduce repeated label/control layout:

```ts
@Builder
textField(label: string, value: string, componentId: string) {
  Column({ space: 6 }) {
    Text(label)
    TextInput({ text: value })
      .id(componentId)
      .onChange((next: string) => {
        if (componentId === 'profile-name-input') {
          this.name = next
        }
      })
  }
  .width('100%')
}
```

For larger fields with independent state or multiple callbacks, use a custom component instead of a builder.

For sliders with nearby live text, use `live-slider-control.md`; do not hide live value ownership in a builder parameter.

## Avoid

- Styled `Text` or `Row` acting as controls when a native control exists.
- Anonymous object literals for form state.
- One large `onClick` that validates, mutates, persists, shows dialogs, and navigates.
- Editing parent state directly from many nested controls without `@Link` or an explicit callback.
- `Slider({ value })` or `TextInput({ text: value })` in a `@Builder` when `value` is a plain parameter and nearby text is expected to refresh while editing.
- Missing empty/error/disabled states for save and submit actions.

content.content:
1	# List And Lazy Data Pattern
2	
3	Use this for repeated rows, feeds, task lists, search results, settings rows, history, notifications, and sections.
4	
5	## Small Fixed Lists
6	
7	Use `List` plus `ForEach` for small lists where all rows can be built eagerly.
8	
9	```ts
10	List({ space: 8 }) {
11	  ForEach(this.items, (item: ItemModel) => {
12	    ListItem() {
13	      ItemRow({ item: item })
14	    }
15	    .id(`item-row-${item.id}`)
16	  }, (item: ItemModel) => item.id)
17	}
18	```
19	
20	Always provide a stable key generator. Prefer model ids over array indexes.
21	
22	## Large Or Unknown Lists
23	
24	Use `LazyForEach` with an `IDataSource` for large, paged, or frequently changing data.
25	
26	```ts
27	class ItemDataSource implements IDataSource {
28	  private items: ItemModel[] = []
29	  private listeners: DataChangeListener[] = []
30	
31	  totalCount(): number {
32	    return this.items.length
33	  }
34	
35	  getData(index: number): ItemModel {
36	    return this.items[index]
37	  }
38	
39	  registerDataChangeListener(listener: DataChangeListener): void {
40	    this.listeners.push(listener)
41	  }
42	
43	  unregisterDataChangeListener(listener: DataChangeListener): void {
44	    this.listeners = this.listeners.filter((item: DataChangeListener) => item !== listener)
45	  }
46	
47	  replaceAll(items: ItemModel[]): void {
48	    this.items = items
49	    this.listeners.forEach((listener: DataChangeListener) => listener.onDataReloaded())
50	  }
51	}
52	```
53	
54	```ts
55	List({ space: 8 }) {
56	  LazyForEach(this.dataSource, (item: ItemModel) => {
57	    ListItem() {
58	      ItemRow({ item: item })
59	    }
60	    .id(`item-row-${item.id}`)
61	  }, (item: ItemModel) => item.id)
62	}
63	.cachedCount(5)
64	```
65	
66	## Sections
67	
68	Use `ListItemGroup` when rows are naturally grouped:
69	
70	```ts
71	List() {
72	  ForEach(this.groups, (group: GroupModel) => {
73	    ListItemGroup({ header: this.groupHeader(group.title) }) {
74	      ForEach(group.items, (item: ItemModel) => {
75	        ListItem() {
76	          ItemRow({ item: item })
77	        }
78	      }, (item: ItemModel) => item.id)
79	    }
80	  }, (group: GroupModel) => group.id)
81	}
82	```
83	
84	## Rules
85	
86	- A generated `List` should produce `ListItem` or `ListItemGroup` for row content.
87	- `LazyForEach` item generator must create exactly one child component per iteration.
88	- Keep `keyGenerator`, `itemGenerator`, and `getData` cheap.
89	- Precompute filtering, sorting, grouping, and formatting outside list generators.
90	- Avoid same-axis nested scroll/list containers unless the inner list has an explicit main-axis size.
91	
92	## Avoid
93	
94	- `ForEach(this.items, ...)` without a key generator.
95	- Keys based on `JSON.stringify(item)` or mutable display text.
96	- Multiple sibling components from one lazy item generator.
97	- Heavy row construction that fetches data or mutates state while rendering.
98	- Wrapping every row in a custom component that is not ultimately placed inside `ListItem`.
99	
100	## Source Notes
101	
102	HarmonyOS List docs distinguish eager `List + ForEach` from lazy loading with `List + LazyForEach`, and note that same-axis nested scroll containers without main-axis size can disable lazy behavior. LazyForEach docs require one child component per item generator and recommend stable custom keys.
103	
104

1	# Search And Filter Pattern
2	
3	Use this for native search boxes, filter chips, scoped search, typeahead, and result refresh.
4	
5	## Core Pattern
6	
7	Keep query and scope state local to the screen. Run filtering in a method, not in `build()`.
8	
9	```ts
10	enum SearchScope {
11	  All,
12	  Active,
13	  Archived
14	}
15	
16	@State private query: string = ''
17	@State private scope: SearchScope = SearchScope.All
18	@State private results: ItemModel[] = []
19	@State private searchPhase: LoadPhase = LoadPhase.Idle
20	
21	private applyLocalSearch(): void {
22	  let normalized: string = this.query.trim().toLowerCase()
23	  this.results = this.repository.filterItems(normalized, this.scope)
24	  this.searchPhase = this.results.length === 0 ? LoadPhase.Empty : LoadPhase.Loaded
25	}
26	```
27	
28	```ts
29	Search({ value: $$this.query, placeholder: 'Search' })
30	  .id('search-input')
31	  .searchButton('Search')
32	  .onChange((value: string) => {
33	    this.query = value
34	    this.applyLocalSearch()
35	  })
36	  .onSubmit((value: string) => {
37	    this.query = value
38	    this.applyLocalSearch()
39	  })
40	```
41	
42	## Async Search
43	
44	- Store a monotonically increasing request id or snapshot query before awaiting.
45	- Ignore stale results if the query or scope changed before the request returned.
46	- Increment the request id when the page disappears, the query is cleared, or a new search starts.
47	- Do not search empty strings unless the product requires "all results".
48	- Debounce typeahead remote search; immediate local filtering is fine.
49	- Show loading, empty, and error states using `loading-placeholders.md`.
50	
51	```ts
52	private searchToken: number = 0
53	private isActive: boolean = false
54	
55	private async runRemoteSearch(): Promise<void> {
56	  let token: number = ++this.searchToken
57	  let query: string = this.query.trim()
58	  let scope: SearchScope = this.scope
59	  if (query.length === 0) {
60	    this.searchToken++
61	    this.results = []
62	    this.searchPhase = LoadPhase.Empty
63	    return
64	  }
65	
66	  this.searchPhase = LoadPhase.Loading
67	  try {
68	    let next: ItemModel[] = await this.client.search(query, scope)
69	    if (!this.isActive || token !== this.searchToken || query !== this.query.trim() || scope !== this.scope) {
70	      return
71	    }
72	    this.results = next
73	    this.searchPhase = next.length === 0 ? LoadPhase.Empty : LoadPhase.Loaded
74	  } catch (error) {
75	    if (this.isActive && token === this.searchToken) {
76	      this.searchPhase = LoadPhase.Error
77	    }
78	  }
79	}
80	```
81	
82	```ts
83	aboutToAppear(): void {
84	  this.isActive = true
85	}
86	
87	aboutToDisappear(): void {
88	  this.isActive = false
89	  this.searchToken++
90	}
91	```
92	
93	For debounced remote search, keep the timer id as a component field and clear it before scheduling a new run and in `aboutToDisappear`.
94	
95	## Scope Controls
96	
97	Use a small native control set for scopes: `SegmentButton` when available in the project, otherwise `Select`, `Radio`, or a short row of real `Button` controls. Do not use styled `Text` as a fake filter control.
98	
99	## Avoid
100	
101	- Filtering or sorting arrays directly in `build()`.
102	- Mutating the source list while the user is typing; write filtered output to a separate state field.
103	- Running remote search on every character without debounce, submit, or stale result protection.
104	- Letting an earlier request replace results for a newer query or scope.
105	- Using localized labels as ids or route params.
106	
107	## Source Notes
108	
109	HarmonyOS Search docs support `Search({ value, placeholder })`, two-way binding for `value`, `onChange`, `onSubmit`, and `searchButton`. Pair this with `async-state.md` for remote search.
110

1	# Build And Smoke Verification
2	
3	Use this after creating or refactoring ArkUI code. It is the closest HarmonyOS equivalent to SwiftUI build, preview-state, and interaction sanity checks.
4	
5	## Minimal Loop
6	
7	1. Run the project build or the available HarmonyOS build command before expanding the change further.
8	2. Fix the first compile error at its source, not by changing unrelated callsites.
9	3. Rebuild before continuing to runtime smoke checks.
10	4. Launch the app when a device/emulator path is available.
11	5. Smoke the primary screen, navigation, dialogs, search/filter, and repeated content.
12	6. Check that state changes propagate correctly and that important loading, empty, and error states can render.
13	
14	## Compile Checks
15	
16	Look specifically for:
17	
18	- Anonymous object type errors in `@State`, props, route params, or list items.
19	- Missing stable key generators in `ForEach`, `LazyForEach`, and `Repeat`.
20	- Invalid child types under `Tabs`, `List`, or `Grid`.
21	- Navigation calls from lifecycle code that should be event-driven.
22	- Builders or lazy generators returning inconsistent child shapes.
23	- Missing imports for model, service, or component files created during split.
24	
25	## Preview-State Checks
26	
27	When a page has meaningful secondary states, create deterministic fixtures or mock services so loaded, loading, empty, and error content can be exercised without live network or auth dependencies.
28	
29	Keep fixtures small and close to the feature until they become shared enough to deserve a helper file. Do not make required production dependencies optional just to make a smoke check easier.
30	
31	## Smoke Checks
32	
33	- Home renders without blank screen.
34	- Primary tabs switch and preserve state when expected.
35	- Navigation push/pop works for detail/edit pages.
36	- Search/filter returns loaded, empty, and cleared states.
37	- Dialogs open, update dynamic content, and close.
38	- Lists/grids render more than one item and row taps hit the intended target.
39	- Loading, empty, and error states are visible when triggered.
40	
41	## Repair Order
42	
43	1. Syntax and type errors.
44	2. Invalid ArkUI child hierarchy.
45	3. State ownership and binding errors.
46	4. Navigation and dialog lifecycle errors.
47	5. Smoke-test failures.
48	6. Visual polish.
49	
50	## Avoid
51	
52	- Continuing feature work with a known compile error.
53	- Fixing symptoms by flattening all components back into `Index.ets`.
54	- Removing accessibility text or useful test hooks to pass a quick build.
55	- Replacing native components with styled text/rows to avoid child constraints.
56	
57	## Source Notes
58	
59	This file intentionally describes process, not a single command, because HarmonyOS projects in this repo may use different hvigor, DevEco, emulator, or codegenie verification paths. Use the available project workflow and keep the repair order stable.
60

file.content:
# Build And Smoke Verification

Use this after creating or refactoring ArkUI code. It is the closest HarmonyOS equivalent to SwiftUI build, preview-state, and interaction sanity checks.

## Minimal Loop

1. Run the project build or the available HarmonyOS build command before expanding the change further.
2. Fix the first compile error at its source, not by changing unrelated callsites.
3. Rebuild before continuing to runtime smoke checks.
4. Launch the app when a device/emulator path is available.
5. Smoke the primary screen, navigation, dialogs, search/filter, and repeated content.
6. Check that state changes propagate correctly and that important loading, empty, and error states can render.

## Compile Checks

Look specifically for:

- Anonymous object type errors in `@State`, props, route params, or list items.
- Missing stable key generators in `ForEach`, `LazyForEach`, and `Repeat`.
- Invalid child types under `Tabs`, `List`, or `Grid`.
- Navigation calls from lifecycle code that should be event-driven.
- Builders or lazy generators returning inconsistent child shapes.
- Missing imports for model, service, or component files created during split.

## Preview-State Checks

When a page has meaningful secondary states, create deterministic fixtures or mock services so loaded, loading, empty, and error content can be exercised without live network or auth dependencies.

Keep fixtures small and close to the feature until they become shared enough to deserve a helper file. Do not make required production dependencies optional just to make a smoke check easier.

## Smoke Checks

- Home renders without blank screen.
- Primary tabs switch and preserve state when expected.
- Navigation push/pop works for detail/edit pages.
- Search/filter returns loaded, empty, and cleared states.
- Dialogs open, update dynamic content, and close.
- Lists/grids render more than one item and row taps hit the intended target.
- Loading, empty, and error states are visible when triggered.

## Repair Order

1. Syntax and type errors.
2. Invalid ArkUI child hierarchy.
3. State ownership and binding errors.
4. Navigation and dialog lifecycle errors.
5. Smoke-test failures.
6. Visual polish.

## Avoid

- Continuing feature work with a known compile error.
- Fixing symptoms by flattening all components back into `Index.ets`.
- Removing accessibility text or useful test hooks to pass a quick build.
- Replacing native components with styled text/rows to avoid child constraints.

## Source Notes

This file intentionally describes process, not a single command, because HarmonyOS projects in this repo may use different hvigor, DevEco, emulator, or codegenie verification paths. Use the available project workflow and keep the repair order stable.

export enum IssueType {
  HAZARD = 'hazard',
  ROUTINE = 'routine',
  RECTIFICATION = 'rectification'
}

export enum RiskLevel {
  LOW = 'low',
  MEDIUM = 'medium',
  HIGH = 'high',
  CRITICAL = 'critical'
}

export enum IssueStatus {
  PENDING = 'pending',
  PROCESSING = 'processing',
  AWAITING_REVIEW = 'awaiting-review',
  COMPLETED = 'completed',
  CLOSED = 'closed'
}

export enum PatrolPointStatus {
  PENDING = 'pending',
  DONE = 'done',
  ABNORMAL = 'abnormal'
}

export enum ContactGroup {
  FIRE = 'fire',
  SECURITY = 'security',
  MEDICAL = 'medical',
  MANAGER = 'manager',
  OTHER = 'other'
}

export enum StatsPeriod {
  DAY = 'day',
  WEEK = 'week',
  MONTH = 'month'
}

export class TimelineEvent {
  id: string = ''
  issueId: string = ''
  timestamp: number = 0
  status: IssueStatus = IssueStatus.PENDING
  note: string = ''
  actorId: string = ''
}

export class Issue {
  id: string = ''
  title: string = ''
  type: IssueType = IssueType.HAZARD
  riskLevel: RiskLevel = RiskLevel.LOW
  status: IssueStatus = IssueStatus.PENDING
  reporterId: string = ''
  assignee: string = ''
  location: string = ''
  photos: string[] = []
  description: string = ''
  createdAt: number = 0
  dueDate: number = 0
  updatedAt: number = 0
  timeline: TimelineEvent[] = []
}

export class PatrolPoint {
  id: string = ''
  name: string = ''
  area: string = ''
  sequence: number = 0
  status: PatrolPointStatus = PatrolPointStatus.PENDING
  lastCheckAt: number | null = null
  lat: number | null = null
  lng: number | null = null
}

export class PatrolRecord {
  id: string = ''
  pointId: string = ''
  checkedAt: number = 0
  photos: string[] = []
  notes: string = ''
  abnormal: boolean = false
  relatedIssueId: string | null = null
}

export class Contact {
  id: string = ''
  name: string = ''
  role: string = ''
  group: ContactGroup = ContactGroup.OTHER
  phone: string = ''
  priority: boolean = false
}

export class TrendPoint {
  label: string = ''
  created: number = 0
  resolved: number = 0
}

export class StatsSnapshot {
  period: StatsPeriod = StatsPeriod.WEEK
  issueCount: number = 0
  resolvedCount: number = 0
  completionRate: number = 0
  patrolCompletionRate: number = 0
  riskDistribution: Record<string, number> = {}
  avgResolutionHours: number = 0
  trend: TrendPoint[] = []
}

export class IssueFormDraft {
  title: string = ''
  type: IssueType = IssueType.HAZARD
  riskLevel: RiskLevel = RiskLevel.LOW
  location: string = ''
  assignee: string = ''
  dueDate: number = 0
  description: string = ''
  photos: string[] = []
  linkedPointId: string | null = null
}

export class ContactFormDraft {
  id: string | null = null
  name: string = ''
  role: string = ''
  group: ContactGroup = ContactGroup.OTHER
  phone: string = ''
  priority: boolean = false
}

export class IssuesFilter {
  query: string = ''
  status: IssueStatus | 'all' = 'all'
  risk: RiskLevel | 'all' = 'all'
}

export class PatrolCheckinDraft {
  pointId: string = ''
  notes: string = ''
  isAbnormal: boolean = false
  photos: string[] = []
}

export class TodaySummary {
  dateLabel: string = ''
  patrolProgress: number = 0
  pendingIssues: number = 0
  resolvedThisWeek: number = 0
}
EOF

let counter: number = 0

export function uuid(): string {
  counter++
  return `${Date.now().toString(36)}-${counter.toString(36)}-${Math.floor(Math.random() * 1000000).toString(36)}`
}
EOF

input.command:
cat > entry/src/main/ets/components/sheets/PatrolCheckinSheet.ets <<'EOF'
import { SafetyAppState } from '../../viewmodels/SafetyAppState'
import { Ids } from '../../common/Ids'
import { Theme } from '../../common/Theme'

@Component
export struct PatrolCheckinSheet {
  @ObjectLink state: SafetyAppState
  onCancel: () => void = () => {}
  onReport: (pointId: string) => void = () => {}

  build() {
    Column({ space: 14 }) {
      Text('巡检打卡')
        .fontSize(18)
        .fontWeight(FontWeight.Bold)
        .fontColor(Theme.brandDeep)
        .width('100%')

      TextArea({ text: this.state.patrolCheckin.notes, placeholder: '记录现场情况' })
        .id(Ids.patrolCheckinNotesInput)
        .width('100%')
        .height(100)
        .borderRadius(12)
        .backgroundColor(Theme.surfaceSecondary)
        .onChange((v: string) => this.state.patrolCheckin.notes = v)

      Row() {
        Text('发现异常')
          .fontSize(14)
          .fontColor(Theme.textPrimary)
        Toggle({ type: ToggleType.Switch, isOn: this.state.patrolCheckin.isAbnormal })
          .id(Ids.patrolCheckinAbnormalToggle)
          .selectedColor(Theme.danger)
          .onChange((v: boolean) => this.state.patrolCheckin.isAbnormal = v)
      }
      .width('100%')
      .height(48)
      .justifyContent(FlexAlign.SpaceBetween)

      Button('拍照')
        .id(Ids.patrolCheckinPhotoButton)
        .fontSize(13)
        .fontWeight(FontWeight.Medium)
        .fontColor(Theme.brandPrimary)
        .width('100%')
        .height(40)
        .borderRadius(12)
        .backgroundColor(Theme.surfaceSecondary)
        .onClick(() => this.state.capturePhotoForCheckin())

      if (this.state.patrolCheckin.photos.length > 0) {
        Row({ space: 8 }) {
          ForEach(this.state.patrolCheckin.photos, (uri: string, index: number) => {
            Image(uri)
              .width(64)
              .height(64)
              .borderRadius(12)
              .objectFit(ImageFit.Cover)
          }, (uri: string, index: number) => `${uri}-${index}`)
        }
        .width('100%')
      }

      Row({ space: 10 }) {
        Button('取消')
          .id(Ids.patrolCheckinCancelButton)
          .fontSize(14)
          .fontColor(Theme.textSecondary)
          .layoutWeight(1)
          .height(46)
          .borderRadius(23)
          .backgroundColor(Theme.surfaceSecondary)
          .onClick(() => this.onCancel())
        Button('提交')
          .id(Ids.patrolCheckinSubmitButton)
          .fontSize(14)
          .fontWeight(FontWeight.Bold)
          .fontColor('#FFFFFF')
          .layoutWeight(1)
          .height(46)
          .borderRadius(23)
          .backgroundColor(Theme.brandPrimary)
          .onClick(() => this.submit())
      }
      .width('100%')
    }
    .id(Ids.patrolCheckinModal)
    .width('100%')
    .padding(20)
    .backgroundColor(Theme.surfacePrimary)
    .borderRadius({ topLeft: 20, topRight: 20 })
  }

  private submit(): void {
    this.state.submitPatrolCheckin()
    if (this.state.patrolCheckin.isAbnormal) {
      this.onReport(this.state.patrolCheckin.pointId)
    }
    this.onCancel()
  }
}
EOF

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/huaweiide/Desktop/fe/code/harmony-pilot/skills/arkts-lint-build-repair

# ArkTS Lint Build Repair

Use after `CheckArktsFiles`, `CompileArkTS`, `build_project`, or `hvigor assembleApp` reports diagnostics. Goal: fix root causes, not only silence the first error.

## Core Loop

1. Capture exact diagnostics: file, line, severity, code, message.
2. Group by root cause; fix one class across all affected files.
3. Do not treat `ok: true` from `CheckArktsFiles` as clean if diagnostics include `severity: Error`.
4. Do not call warnings harmless until checking whether they affect runtime state or build registration.
5. Rerun the same check; if `main_pages.json`, resources, `module.json5`, entry pages, or imports changed, run a real project build.
6. Report remaining warnings separately from build success.

## Route By Diagnostic

- `main_pages.json`, `@Entry`, "must have one and only one '@Entry'", page registration, real `assembleApp`: read `references/entry-build.md`.
- `arkts-no-untyped-obj-literals`, `arkts-no-obj-literals-as-types`, `arkts-no-any-unknown`, private access, missing method, `FontWeight.SemiBold`, `GridItem.justifyContent`, `placeholder`, `noNonUISyntax`: read `references/arkts-types-dsl.md`.
- `Property 'x' has no initializer`, `arkts-no-definite-assignment`, `vm!`, `@Prop`, `@Link`, `@ObjectLink`, shared state not refreshing: read `references/component-state-inputs.md`.
- Repeated failures, build tool discovery, when to rerun `CheckArktsFiles` vs `hvigor`: read `references/verification-loop.md`.

## Hard Rules

- Never fix component input initialization by defaulting to `vm!` / `viewModel!` when UI renders mutable fields from that object.
- Never register tab child components in `main_pages.json`; only registered page entries get `@Entry`.
- Never assume `which hvigor` is sufficient. Check DevEco hvigor paths and project-level build tasks.
- Never stop at static check after entry/profile/resource changes; run the real build.
- Never introduce anonymous object return types in generated ArkTS; use named classes/interfaces.

## Fast Triage Table

| Message fragment | Load | Preferred repair |
|---|---|---|
| `must have one and only one '@Entry'` | `entry-build.md` | Keep only real entry pages in `main_pages.json`; shell `Index` for Tabs |
| `Object literal must correspond...` | `arkts-types-dsl.md` | Named class/interface; align callee parameter type |
| `Object literals cannot be used as type declarations` | `arkts-types-dsl.md` | Replace inline `{ x: T }` type with named type |
| `Only UI component syntax can be written here` | `arkts-types-dsl.md` | Move `let/return/loops` out of `build/@Builder` UI blocks |
| `Property 'SemiBold' does not exist` | `arkts-types-dsl.md` | Use `FontWeight.Medium` or `FontWeight.Bold` |
| `GridItemAttribute.justifyContent` | `arkts-types-dsl.md` | Put `Column/Row` inside `GridItem`, align inner container |
| `Property 'vm' has no initializer` | `component-state-inputs.md` | Choose `@Prop/@Link/@ObjectLink/callback`; do not default to `!` |
| `arkts-no-definite-assignment` | `component-state-inputs.md` | Treat as unresolved ownership warning for dynamic UI |
| `hvigor not found` | `verification-loop.md` | Try DevEco `hvigorw`, project tasks, or build connector |

content.content:
1	# Component State And Inputs Repair
2	
3	## Initializer Diagnostics
4	
5	Messages:
6	
7	```text
8	Property 'vm' has no initializer and is not definitely assigned in the constructor
9	Definite assignment assertions are not supported (arkts-no-definite-assignment)
10	```
11	
12	Do not automatically change:
13	
14	```ts
15	vm: AppViewModel
16	```
17	
18	to:
19	
20	```ts
21	vm!: AppViewModel
22	```
23	
24	For UI that renders mutable VM fields, `vm!` silences initialization but does not define ArkUI state ownership and can leave runtime UI stale.
25	
26	## Decision Table
27	
28	| Child needs | Parent owns | Child declaration | Parent call |
29	|---|---|---|---|
30	| read immutable primitive/string | value | `@Prop title: string = ''` | `Child({ title: this.title })` |
31	| edit parent primitive | `@State query` | `@Link query: string` | `Child({ query: $query })` |
32	| observe shared object fields | `@State appState: AppState` where `AppState` is `@Observed` | `@ObjectLink state: AppState` | `Child({ state: this.appState })` |
33	| trigger behavior only | method/service | `onSave: () => void = () => {}` | `Child({ onSave: () => this.save() })` |
34	| service with no UI refresh | plain private service | `service: Service = new Service()` or constructor-owned | `Child({ service: this.service })` |
35	
36	## Shared ViewModel Pattern
37	
38	Prefer VM as command object and observed state as rendered data:
39	
40	```ts
41	@Observed
42	class AppState {
43	  count: number = 0
44	}
45	
46	class AppViewModel {
47	  readonly state: AppState = new AppState()
48	
49	  increment(): void {
50	    this.state.count += 1
51	  }
52	}
53	
54	@Entry
55	@Component
56	struct Index {
57	  private vm: AppViewModel = new AppViewModel()
58	  @State private appState: AppState = this.vm.state
59	
60	  build() {
61	    Child({
62	      state: this.appState,
63	      onIncrement: () => this.vm.increment()
64	    })
65	  }
66	}
67	
68	@Component
69	struct Child {
70	  @ObjectLink state: AppState
71	  onIncrement: () => void = () => {}
72	
73	  build() {
74	    Button(`${this.state.count}`)
75	      .onClick(() => this.onIncrement())
76	  }
77	}
78	```
79	
80	## If Keeping `@ObjectLink vm`
81	
82	Only use when the VM class itself is intentionally an observed state object:
83	
84	```ts
85	@Observed
86	class AppViewModel {
87	  currentStreak: number = 0
88	}
89	
90	@Component
91	struct HomePage {
92	  @ObjectLink vm: AppViewModel
93	}
94	```
95	
96	Do not use `$vm` with `@ObjectLink`; pass object reference:
97	
98	```ts
99	HomePage({ vm: this.vm })
100	```
101	
102	Use `$name` for `@Link` bindings, not for `@ObjectLink`.
103	
104	## Warning Severity
105	
106	Treat `arkts-no-definite-assignment` as high-risk when:
107	
108	- prop name is `vm`, `viewModel`, `state`, `navStack`, or route params
109	- component reads mutable fields in `build()`
110	- button click mutates the same object
111	- runtime state propagation is required
112	
113	It may be acceptable only for non-reactive injected services after confirming no rendered field depends on internal mutation.
114

1	# ArkTS Types And ArkUI DSL Repair
2	
3	## Object Literal Type Rules
4	
5	Messages:
6	
7	```text
8	Object literal must correspond to some explicitly declared class or interface
9	Object literals cannot be used as type declarations
10	Use explicit types instead of "any", "unknown"
11	```
12	
13	Do:
14	
15	```ts
16	export interface MonthRef {
17	  year: number
18	  month: number
19	}
20	
21	export class DailyRecordJson {
22	  date: string = ''
23	  drank: boolean = false
24	  note: string = ''
25	}
26	
27	nextMonth(year: number, month: number): MonthRef {
28	  return { year: 2026, month: 7 } as MonthRef
29	}
30	```
31	
32	Avoid:
33	
34	```ts
35	nextMonth(): { year: number, month: number } { ... }
36	return { date: this.date, drank: this.drank }
37	```
38	
39	Also align callee types. If a function expects `Record<string, string | number | boolean>`, an interface without an index signature is not assignable. Prefer a named class/interface for fixed shape and change callee type to that shape; use `Record<string, ...>` only for dynamic dictionaries.
40	
41	## Private Access / Missing Method
42	
43	Messages:
44	
45	```text
46	Property 'xService' is private and only accessible within class
47	Property 'computeX' does not exist on type 'ViewModel'
48	```
49	
50	Do not make services public just for UI. Add a small public VM method or computed state:
51	
52	```ts
53	computeMonthRate(year: number, month: number): number {
54	  return this.statsService.computeMonthRate(this.records, year, month)
55	}
56	```
57	
58	## ArkUI UI DSL Restrictions
59	
60	Messages:
61	
62	```text
63	Only UI component syntax can be written here
64	noNonUISyntax
65	```
66	
67	Avoid inside `build()` or `@Builder` UI blocks:
68	
69	```ts
70	let stats = this.countStats()
71	return
72	for (...) {}
73	```
74	
75	Do:
76	
77	```ts
78	@State private soberCount: number = 0
79	
80	private refreshStats(): void {
81	  this.soberCount = this.countSober()
82	}
83	
84	@Builder
85	dayCell(day: CalendarDay) {
86	  if (day.isCurrentMonth) {
87	    Column() { Text(`${day.dayOfMonth}`) }
88	  } else {
89	    Blank()
90	      .width(44)
91	      .height(44)
92	  }
93	}
94	```
95	
96	## Common ArkUI API Corrections
97	
98	`FontWeight.SemiBold` does not exist. Use:
99	
100	```ts
101	FontWeight.Medium
102	FontWeight.Bold
103	```
104	
105	`GridItem` does not support `.justifyContent(...)`. Put an alignable container inside:
106	
107	```ts
108	GridItem() {
109	  Column() {
110	    Text('1')
111	  }
112	  .width('100%')
113	  .height(44)
114	  .justifyContent(FlexAlign.Center)
115	  .alignItems(HorizontalAlign.Center)
116	}
117	```
118	
119	`constraintStyle` is not a standard ArkUI attribute. Usually use:
120	
121	```ts
122	.constraintSize({ maxWidth: 420 })
123	```
124	
125	or parent `.width('100%')` plus content padding.
126	
127	`TextInput.placeholder(...)` / `TextArea.placeholder(...)` are not attribute methods. Use constructor options:
128	
129	```ts
130	TextInput({ text: this.name, placeholder: 'Nickname' })
131	TextArea({ text: this.note, placeholder: 'Note' })
132	```
133	
134	`ForEach` callbacks should be typed and keyed:
135	
136	```ts
137	ForEach(this.days, (day: CalendarDay) => {
138	  DayCell({ day })
139	}, (day: CalendarDay) => day.date)
140	```
141	
142	If index typing causes diagnostics, remove index-dependent styling or precompute row data with named fields.
143

file.content:
# ArkTS Types And ArkUI DSL Repair

## Object Literal Type Rules

Messages:

```text
Object literal must correspond to some explicitly declared class or interface
Object literals cannot be used as type declarations
Use explicit types instead of "any", "unknown"
```

Do:

```ts
export interface MonthRef {
  year: number
  month: number
}

export class DailyRecordJson {
  date: string = ''
  drank: boolean = false
  note: string = ''
}

nextMonth(year: number, month: number): MonthRef {
  return { year: 2026, month: 7 } as MonthRef
}
```

Avoid:

```ts
nextMonth(): { year: number, month: number } { ... }
return { date: this.date, drank: this.drank }
```

Also align callee types. If a function expects `Record<string, string | number | boolean>`, an interface without an index signature is not assignable. Prefer a named class/interface for fixed shape and change callee type to that shape; use `Record<string, ...>` only for dynamic dictionaries.

## Private Access / Missing Method

Messages:

```text
Property 'xService' is private and only accessible within class
Property 'computeX' does not exist on type 'ViewModel'
```

Do not make services public just for UI. Add a small public VM method or computed state:

```ts
computeMonthRate(year: number, month: number): number {
  return this.statsService.computeMonthRate(this.records, year, month)
}
```

## ArkUI UI DSL Restrictions

Messages:

```text
Only UI component syntax can be written here
noNonUISyntax
```

Avoid inside `build()` or `@Builder` UI blocks:

```ts
let stats = this.countStats()
return
for (...) {}
```

Do:

```ts
@State private soberCount: number = 0

private refreshStats(): void {
  this.soberCount = this.countSober()
}

@Builder
dayCell(day: CalendarDay) {
  if (day.isCurrentMonth) {
    Column() { Text(`${day.dayOfMonth}`) }
  } else {
    Blank()
      .width(44)
      .height(44)
  }
}
```

## Common ArkUI API Corrections

`FontWeight.SemiBold` does not exist. Use:

```ts
FontWeight.Medium
FontWeight.Bold
```

`GridItem` does not support `.justifyContent(...)`. Put an alignable container inside:

```ts
GridItem() {
  Column() {
    Text('1')
  }
  .width('100%')
  .height(44)
  .justifyContent(FlexAlign.Center)
  .alignItems(HorizontalAlign.Center)
}
```

`constraintStyle` is not a standard ArkUI attribute. Usually use:

```ts
.constraintSize({ maxWidth: 420 })
```

or parent `.width('100%')` plus content padding.

`TextInput.placeholder(...)` / `TextArea.placeholder(...)` are not attribute methods. Use constructor options:

```ts
TextInput({ text: this.name, placeholder: 'Nickname' })
TextArea({ text: this.note, placeholder: 'Note' })
```

`ForEach` callbacks should be typed and keyed:

```ts
ForEach(this.days, (day: CalendarDay) => {
  DayCell({ day })
}, (day: CalendarDay) => day.date)
```

If index typing causes diagnostics, remove index-dependent styling or precompute row data with named fields.

1	# Entry And Build Repair
2	
3	## main_pages.json / @Entry
4	
5	Rule: every path in `entry/src/main/resources/base/profile/main_pages.json` must point to an ArkTS page file with exactly one `@Entry` component. Imported child components must not be registered.
6	
7	Tabbed shell pattern:
8	
9	```json
10	{
11	  "src": [
12	    "pages/Index"
13	  ]
14	}
15	```
16	
17	```ts
18	@Entry
19	@Component
20	struct Index {
21	  build() {
22	    Tabs() {
23	      TabContent() {
24	        HomePage()
25	      }
26	      TabContent() {
27	        CalendarPage()
28	      }
29	    }
30	  }
31	}
32	
33	@Component
34	export struct HomePage {
35	  build() {}
36	}
37	```
38	
39	Fix message:
40	
41	```text
42	A page configured in 'main_pages.json' must have one and only one '@Entry' decorator
43	```
44	
45	Repair:
46	
47	- If page is a shell route loaded by ability: keep it in `main_pages.json`, ensure exactly one `@Entry`.
48	- If page is used inside `Tabs`, `Navigation`, `NavDestination`, or another component: remove it from `main_pages.json`, keep `@Component`, no `@Entry`.
49	- Do not add `@Entry` to every component just to satisfy build; that breaks app shell ownership.
50	
51	## Build Command
52	
53	Prefer project-level build from project root:
54	
55	```bash
56	/Applications/DevEco-Studio.app/Contents/tools/hvigor/bin/hvigorw assembleApp --no-daemon
57	```
58	
59	Fallback paths to check before saying hvigor is unavailable:
60	
61	```bash
62	test -x /Applications/DevEco-Studio.app/Contents/tools/hvigor/bin/hvigorw
63	test -x /Applications/DevEco-Studio.app/contents/tools/hvigor/bin/hvigorw
64	find /Applications/DevEco-Studio.app/Contents/tools -path '*hvigor*/bin/hvigorw' -type f
65	```
66	
67	Avoid for app assembly unless verified by project tasks:
68	
69	```bash
70	hvigorw --mode module -p module=entry@default assembleApp
71	```
72	
73	That can report task-not-found even when project-level `assembleApp` exists.
74	
75	## When Real Build Is Required
76	
77	Run real build after changing:
78	
79	- `main_pages.json`
80	- `module.json5`
81	- `AppScope/app.json5`
82	- resource JSON under `resources/`
83	- `@Entry` files
84	- imports used only by build/resource processing
85	
86	`CheckArktsFiles` can miss profile registration and packaging errors.
87

file.content:
# Entry And Build Repair

## main_pages.json / @Entry

Rule: every path in `entry/src/main/resources/base/profile/main_pages.json` must point to an ArkTS page file with exactly one `@Entry` component. Imported child components must not be registered.

Tabbed shell pattern:

```json
{
  "src": [
    "pages/Index"
  ]
}
```

```ts
@Entry
@Component
struct Index {
  build() {
    Tabs() {
      TabContent() {
        HomePage()
      }
      TabContent() {
        CalendarPage()
      }
    }
  }
}

@Component
export struct HomePage {
  build() {}
}
```

Fix message:

```text
A page configured in 'main_pages.json' must have one and only one '@Entry' decorator
```

Repair:

- If page is a shell route loaded by ability: keep it in `main_pages.json`, ensure exactly one `@Entry`.
- If page is used inside `Tabs`, `Navigation`, `NavDestination`, or another component: remove it from `main_pages.json`, keep `@Component`, no `@Entry`.
- Do not add `@Entry` to every component just to satisfy build; that breaks app shell ownership.

## Build Command

Prefer project-level build from project root:

```bash
/Applications/DevEco-Studio.app/Contents/tools/hvigor/bin/hvigorw assembleApp --no-daemon
```

Fallback paths to check before saying hvigor is unavailable:

```bash
test -x /Applications/DevEco-Studio.app/Contents/tools/hvigor/bin/hvigorw
test -x /Applications/DevEco-Studio.app/contents/tools/hvigor/bin/hvigorw
find /Applications/DevEco-Studio.app/Contents/tools -path '*hvigor*/bin/hvigorw' -type f
```

Avoid for app assembly unless verified by project tasks:

```bash
hvigorw --mode module -p module=entry@default assembleApp
```

That can report task-not-found even when project-level `assembleApp` exists.

## When Real Build Is Required

Run real build after changing:

- `main_pages.json`
- `module.json5`
- `AppScope/app.json5`
- resource JSON under `resources/`
- `@Entry` files
- imports used only by build/resource processing

`CheckArktsFiles` can miss profile registration and packaging errors.

5	
6	interface PatrolPageProps {

12	@Component
13	export struct PatrolPage {
14	  @ObjectLink state: SafetyAppState

16	  onReportWithPoint: (pointId: string) => void = () => {}
17	
18	  build() {
19	    Column({ space: 0 }) {
20	      Text('巡检打卡')
21	        .id(Ids.patrolHeaderTitle)
22	        .fontSize(22)

32	        .width('100%')

34	
35	      this.ProgressHeader()
36	
37	      List({ space: 10 }) {
38	        ForEach(this.state.patrolPoints, (point: PatrolPoint) => {
39	          ListItem() {
40	            this.PointRow(point)
41	          }
42	          .id(`patrol-point-${point.id}`)
43	        }, (point: PatrolPoint) => point.id)
44	      }
45	      .id(Ids.patrolPointList)
46	      .width('100%')
47	      .layoutWeight(1)
48	      .padding({ left: Theme.pageHorizontalPadding, right: Theme.pageHorizontalPadding })
49	      .scrollBar(BarState.Off)
50	    }
51	    .width('100%')
52	    .height('100%')
53	    .backgroundColor(Theme.pageBg)
54	  }
55	
56	  @Builder
57	  ProgressHeader() {
58	    Row() {
59	      Column({ space: 4 }) {
60	        Text(`${Math.round(this.patrolProgress() * 100)}%`)
61	          .id(Ids.patrolProgressRing)
62	          .fontSize(28)
63	          .fontWeight(FontWeight.Bold)
64	          .fontColor(Theme.brandDeep)
65	        Text('今日巡检完成度')
66	          .fontSize(11)
67	          .fontColor(Theme.textSecondary)
68	      }
69	      .layoutWeight(1)
70	      .alignItems(HorizontalAlign.Start)
71	
72	      Column({ space: 4 }) {
73	        Text('园区常规路线')
74	          .id(Ids.patrolRouteName)
75	          .fontSize(15)
76	          .fontWeight(FontWeight.Medium)
77	          .fontColor(Theme.textPrimary)
78	        Text(`${this.doneCount()}/${this.state.patrolPoints.length} 点位`)
79	          .fontSize(12)
80	          .fontColor(Theme.textTertiary)
81	      }
82	      .alignItems(HorizontalAlign.End)
83	    }
84	    .width('100%')
85	    .padding(16)
86	    .margin({ left: Theme.pageHorizontalPadding, right: Theme.pageHorizontalPadding, bottom: 10 })
87	    .borderRadius(16)

90	    .justifyContent(FlexAlign.SpaceBetween)

93	  @Builder

95	    Row({ space: 12 }) {
96	      Column({ space: 3 }) {

105	      .layoutWeight(1)
106	      .alignItems(HorizontalAlign.Start)
107	
108	      Button('打卡')
109	        .id(`patrol-point-${point.id}-checkin-button`)
110	        .fontSize(12)
111	        .fontWeight(FontWeight.Medium)
112	        .fontColor('#FFFFFF')
113	        .height(32)
114	        .padding({ left: 14, right: 14 })
115	        .borderRadius(16)
116	        .backgroundColor(point.status === PatrolPointStatus.PENDING ? Theme.brandPrimary : Theme.safe)
117	        .enabled(point.status === PatrolPointStatus.PENDING)
118	        .onClick(() => this.onCheckin(point.id))
119	    }
120	    .width('100%')
121	    .padding(14)
122	    .borderRadius(14)
123	    .backgroundColor(Theme.surfacePrimary)
124	    .shadow({ radius: 2, color: 'rgba(15,23,42,0.04)', offsetY: 1 })
125	    .border({ width: { left: 4 }, color: { left: this.pointColor(point.status) } })
126	  }
127	
128	  private pointColor(status: PatrolPointStatus): string {
129	    if (status === PatrolPointStatus.DONE) {
130	      return Theme.safe
131	    } else if (status === PatrolPointStatus.ABNORMAL) {
132	      return Theme.danger
133	    }
134	    return Theme.brandPrimary
135	  }
136	
137	  private doneCount(): number {
138	    return this.state.patrolPoints.filter((p: PatrolPoint) => p.status !== PatrolPointStatus.PENDING).length
139	  }
140	
141	  private patrolProgress(): number {
142	    let total: number = this.state.patrolPoints.length || 1
143	    return this.doneCount() / total
144	  }
145	}
